skill.name: slack
skill.description: Interact with Slack workspaces using browser automation. Use when the user needs to check unread channels, navigate Slack, send messages, extract data, find information, search conversations, or automate any Slack task.
skill.summary: Browser-based Slack automation for checking messages, searching conversations, and extracting data without API authentication.
skill.author: vercel-labs

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 145 patterns primarily related to external_commands (agent-browser CLI tool) and network (Slack URLs). All findings are false positives: agent-browser is a legitimate browser automation tool for Slack interaction, and the flagged URLs point to slack.com - the intended service. No credential exfiltration, code injection, or malicious patterns detected. Risk level downgraded from static analysis suggestion based on contextual review.

low_findings.title: Browser automation tool usage
low_findings.description: Skill uses agent-browser CLI for Slack interaction. This is the intended design pattern for browser-based automation, not a security vulnerability. Commands operate within user's authenticated Slack session.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The agent-browser tool is explicitly declared in allowed-tools header and used consistently for legitimate Slack automation tasks.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Slack URLs
low_findings.description: References to app.slack.com and slack.com/help are the intended target service URLs for browser automation, not external data exfiltration endpoints.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: URLs point to official Slack domains which are the legitimate target of this automation skill.

content.value_statement: Manually checking Slack messages and searching conversations takes time. This skill automates Slack interaction through browser automation without requiring API authentication or bot tokens.
actual_capabilities: Check unread messages across channels and direct messages
Navigate Slack workspace using browser automation
Search conversations with keywords and filters
Extract message content, timestamps, and user information
Capture screenshots of Slack conversations and channels
Monitor specific channels for new activity
limitations: Requires an existing authenticated Slack browser session
Cannot access Slack API features like sending messages via API
Limited to one workspace per browser session
Screenshots and snapshots are tied to the current session state

use_cases.title: Daily Unread Summary
use_cases.description: Automatically check all unread messages across channels and DMs each morning to prioritize responses.
use_cases.target_user: Team members managing multiple Slack channels

use_cases.title: Conversation Search and Archive
use_cases.description: Search for historical messages containing specific keywords and extract relevant conversation threads for documentation.
use_cases.target_user: Project managers tracking decisions and action items

use_cases.title: Channel Activity Monitoring
use_cases.description: Monitor specific channels for new messages or mentions and capture screenshots of important discussions.
use_cases.target_user: Engineering leads staying updated on team communications

prompt_templates.title: Check Unread Messages
prompt_templates.prompt: Check my Slack for unread messages. Show me which channels and direct messages have new activity, and capture screenshots of the unread indicators.
prompt_templates.scenario: Starting your workday and need to catch up on overnight messages

prompt_templates.title: Search for Keywords
prompt_templates.prompt: Search Slack for messages containing "deployment" from the last week. Extract the sender names, timestamps, and message content from the top 5 results.
prompt_templates.scenario: Looking for information about recent deployments discussed in team channels

prompt_templates.title: Monitor Channel Activity
prompt_templates.prompt: Navigate to the #engineering channel and check for new messages since 9 AM. Capture the conversation thread for any discussions about the production issue.
prompt_templates.scenario: Tracking ongoing incident discussion in a specific channel

prompt_templates.title: Extract Conversation Data
prompt_templates.prompt: Find all messages in #product-design where @alice mentioned "deadline" or "timeline" this month. Extract the full message text, timestamps, and any attached files into a structured summary.
prompt_templates.scenario: Preparing a report on project timeline discussions for stakeholder review

output: Found 12 messages matching "deployment" in the last 7 days
1. @bob in #engineering: Deployment scheduled for 2 PM today (Today 10:30 AM)
2. @charlie in #devops: Production deployment completed successfully (Yesterday 4:15 PM)
3. @alice in #engineering: Rollback plan documented in Confluence (Feb 28, 9:45 AM)

best_practices: Connect to existing Slack sessions using port 9222 for faster access
Take snapshots before clicking to identify element references
Re-snapshot after navigation to refresh element references
Use JSON snapshots for programmatic data extraction
Add delays between commands to allow UI updates
anti_patterns: Do not attempt rapid-fire commands without delays - Slack may rate-limit
Do not assume element references persist across sessions - always snapshot first
Do not use for cross-workspace automation - limited to current authenticated workspace
Do not rely on screenshots for long-term storage - capture data via JSON snapshots

faq.question: Do I need Slack API credentials to use this skill?
faq.answer: No. This skill uses browser automation to interact with Slack's web interface. It works with your existing authenticated browser session, requiring no API keys, OAuth tokens, or bot setup.

faq.question: Can this skill send messages to Slack?
faq.answer: This skill focuses on reading and extracting data from Slack via browser automation. Sending messages requires simulating keyboard input through the browser interface rather than using the Slack API.

faq.question: How do I connect to my Slack workspace?
faq.answer: If Slack is already open in your browser, use 'agent-browser connect 9222' to connect to the existing session. If not, use 'agent-browser open https://app.slack.com' and authenticate manually.

faq.question: Can I automate multiple Slack workspaces?
faq.answer: Each browser session is limited to one workspace at a time. To work with multiple workspaces, you need separate browser sessions or profiles for each workspace.

faq.question: What happens if Slack logs me out during automation?
faq.answer: If your session expires, the automation will fail. You need to manually re-authenticate in the browser and reconnect using 'agent-browser connect 9222'.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to use with sensitive conversations?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill operates within your authenticated session and does not transmit data externally. Screenshots and snapshots are stored locally. However, handle captured data according to your organization's security policies.
